summary: A chat app for a user and their friends to communicate, with plans to add a friends/contacts system including adding friends by username, seeing who's online, and private chats. FaceTime (video calling) feature has been requested.
known_issues: Remote audio not audible during video calls (Version 7)

existing_features.title: Build a chat UI that displays a scrollable feed of messages showing the sender name, message text, and time sent. Include a text input and a send button at the bottom to submit new messages. The user must enter a display name before chatting.

Apply a warm, modern visual theme with a bold color palette (e.g., coral/amber tones), clean card-based layout, rounded chat bubbles, and friendly sans-serif typography. The design should feel social, approachable, and consistent throughout the app.

Add a Friends panel to the chat UI where the user can search for other users by username, send friend requests, view their friend list with online/offline status indicators, and open a private chat with any friend.

Prepare the app for publishing to the Caffeine App Market by adding a descriptive app name ('Friends Chat'), a short tagline, and an app description that summarizes the app's core features (public chat, friends list, private messaging, online status).

In the chat message feed, display the full sender display name without truncation. The sender name element in each message bubble (covering both own and other-sender bubbles) must show the complete name, removing any CSS text truncation, overflow hidden, or max-width constraints that cut off the name.

Add a video calling feature accessible from the Friends panel and Private Chat view. Each friend list item and private chat header should display a video call button. Clicking it opens a full-screen or modal video call UI showing the local camera feed and a placeholder remote video area (since true peer-to-peer WebRTC is not available, simulate the UI with a 'Calling…' / 'Connected' state using the local camera stream). Include controls for muting the microphone, toggling the camera on/off, and ending the call. The UI should match the existing warm coral/amber theme.
Audit and fix bugs in the public chat message feed: ensure messages load correctly, sending a new message works without errors, and the message feed auto-scrolls to the latest message after posting.

Audit and fix bugs in the Friends panel: user search by username returns correct results, sending a friend request succeeds, incoming friend requests can be accepted or declined, and the friend list displays with accurate online/offline status indicators.

Audit and fix bugs in the private chat view: opening a private chat with a friend loads the correct message thread, sending messages in a private thread works, and the view auto-scrolls to the latest message.

Audit and fix bugs in the video call modal: the video call button in both the Friends panel and the Private Chat view opens the VideoCallModal correctly, the local camera stream initialises without errors, mute/camera toggle controls function as expected, and ending the call properly cleans up the stream and closes the modal.

Audit and fix bugs in the user profile setup flow: the DisplayNamePrompt and ProfileSetupModal correctly create or retrieve a user profile on first launch, and returning users are not re-prompted unnecessarily.

Audit and fix all bugs in the video call feature end-to-end: the video call button in both FriendListItem (Friends panel) and PrivateChatView header must correctly open the VideoCallModal; the useVideoCall hook must successfully request and initialise the local camera/microphone media stream without errors; mute (audio) and camera toggle controls must reflect the correct state and actually mute/unmute or enable/disable the corresponding media track; ending the call must stop all media tracks, release the stream, and close/unmount the VideoCallModal cleanly; and the simulated 'Calling…' → 'Connected' state transition must work as intended. Ensure no unhandled promise rejections or console errors occur during the full call lifecycle.

Fix the video call feature so that the remote party's audio is audible during a call. The VideoCallModal must correctly attach the remote media stream (including audio tracks) to the remote video/audio element so the local user can hear the other person. Ensure the audio element or video element used for remote playback has its srcObject set to the remote stream, is not muted, and that autoPlay is set. Also verify that getUserMedia requests audio: true so the local microphone track is captured and available for the simulated remote audio path.

new_feature_requests.summary: Chat app for friends to communicate with each other
Create a backend actor that stores chat messages with a sender name, message text, and timestamp. Provide functions to post a new message and retrieve all messages in chronological order.
Add a friends/contacts system: users can add friends by username, see who's online, and have private chats.

Extend the backend actor to support a friends/contacts system: store registered users (by display name/username), allow users to send and accept friend requests, track online presence (last seen timestamp), and support private one-on-one message threads between two users.
Set up the app for publishing to the App Market.
Add FaceTime-style video calling feature to the app.

Fix any backend Motoko actor issues: ensure all public functions (postMessage, getMessages, createUserProfile, getUserProfile, sendFriendRequest, acceptFriendRequest, declineFriendRequest, getPrivateThread, sendPrivateMessage, updateOnlineStatus) return correct results and do not trap or throw unexpected errors under normal usage.
